content: AWQ vs GPTQ vs sparsity+distill: AWQ finds not all weights equal; protects ~1% salient channels identified by ACTIVATION distribution (not weight magnitude) via per-channel scaling absorbed upstream; no backprop, generalizes across domains/modalities (first multimodal 4-bit). GPTQ optimizes quantized weights directly on INT4 lattice with Hessian-weighted updates. Sparsity+quant hybrids: SLiM integrates uniform quantization + semi-structured 2:4 sparsity + low-rank adapters in one-shot, +5.66% on 2:4 4-bit. Joint structural pruning + mixed-precision quantization (2026) minimizes global error propagation, up to 59-85% lower perplexity than SoTA at 1-3 bits. Spectral-Stream's RND-01 (APoZ/Wanda/SparseGPT importance scoring) aligns with this trend but must run on CPU-only NumPy.
